info.title: Skilljar API
info.description:
The Skilljar API provides comprehensive access to our customer education platform, enabling you to programmatically manage courses, users, enrollments, and more.

This interactive documentation is automatically generated from our codebase and stays current with the latest features and endpoints.

## Getting Started

New to the Skilljar API? Check out these essential resources:

- **[API Getting Started Guide](https://support.gainsight.com/Skilljar/Develop_and_Customize/API/Getting_started_with_the_Skilljar_API)** - Authentication, basic concepts, and your first API call
- **[Token-based SSO](https://support.gainsight.com/Skilljar/Develop_and_Customize/Single_Sign_on_(SSO)/Configuring_Token-Based_Single_Sign-On_(SSO))** - Seamlessly integrate user authentication
- **[Webhooks](https://support.gainsight.com/Skilljar/Develop_and_Customize/API/Using_Webhooks_API)** - Real-time notifications for platform events

get.description:
Retrieve a paginated list of all assets in your organization.

Returns summary information for each asset, including name, size, and type.
Download URLs are not included in this response.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

post.description:
Upload a new asset to your organization.

Accepts a file or remote file URL and creates a new asset record.
The file will be securely downloaded and re-hosted by Skilljar.
The response includes asset metadata, but excludes download URL and type until processing is complete.

get.description:
Retrieve detailed information for a specific asset.

Returns all metadata for the asset, including a signed download URL valid for 1 hour.
Only assets belonging to your organization are accessible.

delete.description:
Permanently delete an asset from your organization.

The asset will be removed if it is not currently used in any lessons or courses.
Returns HTTP 204 No Content on success.
If the asset is in use, returns HTTP 400 Bad Request with an error message.

get.description:
List all courses in your organization.

Returns a paginated list of courses with details such as title, description, labels,
  creation and modification dates, and course time settings. Supports filtering by label name.

parameters.name: label__name
parameters.description: Filter courses by exact match on label name

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

post.description:
Create a new course.

Creates a course with the provided details, including title, description, labels,
  and course time settings. Returns the created course object.

get.description:
Retrieve a single course.

Returns detailed information about a specific course, including title, description, labels,
  creation and modification dates, and course time settings.

put.description:
Update an existing course.

Updates the specified course with the provided data.
Supports partial updates; only the fields you provide will be updated. Returns the updated course object.

patch.description:
Partially update a course.

Updates one or more fields of an existing course.
Only the fields you provide will be updated; all others remain unchanged. Returns the updated course object.

delete.description:
Delete a course and its drafts.

Permanently deletes the specified course and all associated draft versions.
This action cannot be undone. Returns a 204 No Content response if successful.

get.description:
List all labels associated with a course.

Returns a paginated list of label associations for the specified course.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

post.description:
Associate a label with a course.

Creates a new association between the specified label and course.

get.description:
Retrieve a label association for a course.

Returns details for the label association identified by `label_id` for the given course.

delete.description:
Remove a label association from a course.

Deletes the association between the specified label and course.

get.description:
List all domains.

Returns a paginated list of domains for your organization,
  including their names, access settings, and catalog titles.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

get.description:
Retrieve a domain.

Returns details for a specific domain,
  including its name, catalog title, access settings, and marketing messages.

put.description:
Update a domain.

Allows updating one or more fields of a domain. Both PUT and PATCH requests perform partial updates.

patch.description:
Partially update a domain.

Allows updating specific fields of a domain.

get.description:
List access code pools.

Retrieve a paginated list of access code pools for a domain.
Pools can be filtered by name using the `name` query parameter.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter returned access code pools by name

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

post.description:
Create an access code pool.

Create a new, empty pool of access codes for the specified domain.
The pool can be used to manage and distribute access codes to learners.

get.description:
Retrieve an access code pool.

Get details for a specific access code pool, including its name, status, validity period, and code count.

put.description:
Update an access code pool.

Update the properties of an existing access code pool. Both PUT and PATCH requests are supported.

patch.description:
Update an access code pool.

Update the properties of an existing access code pool. Both PUT and PATCH requests are supported.

delete.description:
Delete an access code pool.

Permanently delete an access code pool and all access codes it contains.

get.description:
List access codes.

Retrieve a paginated list of access codes in a pool.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

post.description:
Create an access code.

Add a new access code to a pool. The code can be distributed to learners for domain access.

get.description:
Retrieve an access code.

Get details for a specific access code, including its status, usage limits, and assigned users.

put.description:
Update an access code.

Update the properties of an existing access code. Both PUT and PATCH requests are supported.

patch.description:
Update an access code.

Update the properties of an existing access code. Both PUT and PATCH requests are supported.

delete.description:
Delete an access code.

Permanently remove an access code from the pool.

Manage items in a Catalog Tile Content Block on a Builder Catalog Page.

- **List**: Retrieve all items for a given content on a builder catalog page
- **Retrieve**: Get detailed information about a specific item in a content block
- **Create**: Add a new item to the content block
- **Update**: Modify the order of an existing item inside a content block
- **Destroy**: Remove an item from a given content block

get.description:
Retrieve a paginated list of all course series for the specified domain.

Each course series includes metadata, associated offers, published paths, and published courses.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

get.description:
Retrieve detailed information for a specific course series within the domain.

The response includes metadata, offers, published paths, and the list of published courses in the series.

Retrieve a paginated list of all published courses in the specified course series for this domain.

Each published course includes its order in the series, course details, and related offers.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

get.description:
List all Plans available in the specified Domain.

Returns a paginated list of Plans, including associated Offer details such as pricing
  and registration information.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

get.description:
Retrieve details for a specific Plan within a Domain.

Returns detailed information about the Plan, including its title, associated Offers, and registration URLs.

List all enrollments for a specific Plan.

Returns a paginated list of enrollments.
Each enrollment includes user information, enrollment status, and purchase details if available.
A user may have multiple enrollments for a Plan. Access to a Plan is granted if at least one enrollment
  is active and not expired.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

post.description:
Enroll a user in a Plan.

This operation is idempotent for the combination of user ID and enrollment date.
If an enrollment already exists for the specified user and date, a new enrollment will not be created.
Each call with a new user or enrollment date will create a new enrollment.

The user must already have a domain membership to be enrolled.

get.description:
Retrieve details for a specific Plan enrollment.

Returns enrollment information, including user details, enrollment status, and purchase information if
the enrollment was associated with a purchase or registration through the platform.

put.description:
Update details for a Plan enrollment.

Both PUT and PATCH requests perform partial updates to the enrollment record.

patch.description:
Update details for a Plan enrollment.

Both PUT and PATCH requests perform partial updates to the enrollment record.

get.description:
List all courses published to this domain.

Returns a paginated list of published courses for the domain.
Supports filtering by course ID and searching by keyword.

parameters.name: course__id
parameters.description: Filter by course id, e.g. `course__id=abcd1234`

parameters.name: include_searchable_content
parameters.description: If set to True, the response also includes the following fields ('lesson_list', 'tags', 'short_description', 'long_description_html', 'search_keywords')

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: URL encoded string, return the results and the order as if a catalog search

post.description:
Publish a course to this domain.

Makes a course available to learners on the specified domain.
The course must not already be published to this domain.

get.description:
Retrieve a published course for this domain.

Returns detailed information about a published course, including metadata, publishing settings,
  and catalog ordering.

put.description:
Update a published course in this domain.

Allows partial updates to published course settings, such as visibility, registration, or slug.

patch.description:
Partially update a published course in this domain.

Allows partial updates to published course settings, such as visibility, registration, or slug.

delete.description:
Unpublish a course from this domain.

Removes the course from the domain, making it inaccessible to learners.
This action is irreversible and also removes the course from all associated
  content syndication configurations.

get.description:
List enrollments for a published course in this domain.

Returns a paginated list of enrollments for the specified published course.
Each user may have multiple enrollments. Includes purchase details if available.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

post.description:
Enroll a user in a published course for this domain.

This operation is idempotent for the same user and enrollment time.
The user must have a domain membership to be enrolled.

get.description:
Retrieve enrollment details for a published course in this domain.

Returns information about a specific enrollment, including user and purchase details if applicable.

put.description:
Update enrollment details for a published course in this domain.

Allows partial updates to enrollment information, such as status or expiration.

patch.description:
Update enrollment details for a published course in this domain.

Allows partial updates to enrollment information, such as status or expiration.

delete.description: Unlink a prerequisite from a PublishedCourse. Returns a `204 No Content` response on success.

patch.description:
Update the reenrollment rule for a `PublishedCourse`.

PUT and PATCH are identical - both do partial updates.

get.description:
List all Published Paths for the specified domain.

Returns a paginated list of Published Paths available on the domain. Supports filtering and search.

parameters.name: include_searchable_content
parameters.description: If set to True, the response also includes the following fields ('path_item_list', 'title', 'short_description', 'long_description_html', 'search_keywords' 'tags', 'hidden')

parameters.name: label__name
parameters.description: Filters published paths by exact match on label name

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: URL encoded string, return the results and the order as if a catalog search. Note - This does not search within the path_item_list.

post.description:
Create a new Published Path and its associated Path object.

The created Published Path will have a default Offer attached.
Required and optional fields for the Path object are documented in the schema.

get.description:
Retrieve details for a specific Published Path.

Returns all metadata and configuration for the Published Path, including associated offers and path details.

put.description:
Update a Published Path.

Allows partial updates to Published Path fields, such as the slug. Both PUT and PATCH perform partial updates.

patch.description:
Partially update a Published Path.

Allows updating one or more fields of the Published Path, such as the slug.

delete.description:
Delete a Published Path.

Removes the Published Path and its associated course series from the domain.

get.description: List labels on a published path.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

post.description: Associate a Label association to a Published Path.

get.description: Retrieve label details.

delete.description: Deletes a Label association to a Published Path and returns a `204` response.

get.description:
List Path Items for a Published Path.

Returns a paginated list of Path Items, ordered as configured in the Published Path.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

post.description:
Create a new Path Item in a Published Path.

Adds a course or learning object to the sequence of the Published Path.

get.description:
Retrieve details for a specific Path Item.

Returns all metadata for the Path Item, including its order and associated published course.

put.description:
Update a Path Item.

Allows partial updates to Path Item fields, such as order or associated published course.

patch.description:
Partially update a Path Item.

Allows updating one or more fields of the Path Item.

delete.description:
Delete a Path Item.

Removes the Path Item from the Published Path sequence.

get.description:
List enrollments for a Published Path.

Returns a paginated list of enrollments for the specified Published Path, including user and purchase details.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

post.description:
Enroll a user in a Published Path.

This operation is idempotent for the same user and enrollment time.
Only users with an existing domain membership can be enrolled.

get.description:
Retrieve details for a Published Path enrollment.

Returns enrollment information, including user details and purchase information if applicable.

put.description:
Manage enrollments for a Published Path.

Enrollments represent a user's access to a Published Path.
This API allows you to list, retrieve, create, and update enrollments for a specific Published Path.

Enrollment states:
- A user may have multiple enrollments for a path.
- Access is granted if at least one enrollment is active and not expired.

patch.description:
Manage enrollments for a Published Path.

Enrollments represent a user's access to a Published Path.
This API allows you to list, retrieve, create, and update enrollments for a specific Published Path.

Enrollment states:
- A user may have multiple enrollments for a path.
- Access is granted if at least one enrollment is active and not expired.

get.description: List Tags on a Published Path.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

post.description: Add a Tag to a Published Path.

get.description: Retrieve Tag details.

delete.description: Removes a Tag from a Published Path and returns a `204` response.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

post.description: Create a StudentGroup visibility override for a Published Path

get.description: Retrieve StudentGroup visibility override for PublishedPath.

delete.description: Deletes a StudentGroup visibility override for a PublishedPath and returns a `204` response.

get.description: Manage signup fields for a domain. Signup fields define the information collected from users during the signup process for a specific domain. Each field may have a set of options, validation rules, and display properties. Use this endpoint to view the list of signup fields configured for a domain.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

get.description:
List users for a domain.

Returns a paginated list of users associated with the domain.
  Supports filtering by user details, access code, and signup field data.

parameters.name: access_code
parameters.description: Use a value of "null" to see domain users who accessed the domain without an access code. No other values are accepted for this query.

parameters.name: access_code__code
parameters.description: Filter domain users by access code used - case sensitive

parameters.name: access_code__id
parameters.description: Filter domain users by id of access code used - not case sensitive

parameters.name: access_code__pool__id
parameters.description: Filter domain users by id of access code pool used - not case sensitive

parameters.name: active
parameters.description: Filter domain users by active (true or false)

parameters.name: include_signup_field_data
parameters.description: If set to true, the response also includes the following data for each user ("signup_fields")

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: signup_field_id
parameters.description: Filter domain users by a sign up field ID

parameters.name: signup_field_label
parameters.description: Filter domain users by a sign up field Label - case sensitive

parameters.name: signup_field_value
parameters.description: Filter domain users by a sign up field Value - case sensitive

parameters.name: user__email
parameters.description: Filter domain users by email address

parameters.name: user__first_name
parameters.description: Filter domain users by first name - case sensitive

parameters.name: user__last_name
parameters.description: Filter domain users by last name - case sensitive

post.description:
Add a user to a domain.

If the provided email address already exists, the user will be added to the domain
  and their existing details will be returned.

If the email is new, a new user will be created and added to the domain.

get.description: Retrieve details for a specific user within a domain.

put.description:
Update a user's domain membership.

Only the `active` status, `expires_at` date, and `access_code`
  (which can be removed by setting to null) may be updated. Both PUT and PATCH perform partial updates.

patch.description:
Update a user's domain membership.

Only the `active` status, `expires_at` date, and `access_code`
  (which can be removed by setting to null) may be updated. Both PUT and PATCH perform partial updates.

get.description:
Retrieve a list of all invitations for a specific user within a domain.

Returns all invitations that have been created for the user in the specified domain, ordered by creation date.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

post.description:
Create a new invitation for a user to join a domain.

Sends an invitation email to the user unless `send_email` is set to `False`.
The user must already be a member of the domain before an invitation can be created.

get.description:
Retrieve details for a specific domain user invitation.

Provides detailed information about a single invitation sent to a user for domain membership.

put.description: Grant and update student manager access for a user.

delete.description: Revoke student manager access for a user.

get.description:
Retrieve a list of all signup field values for a user in the specified domain.

Returns a paginated list of all signup field values that have been set for the user.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

post.description:
Set or update the value of a signup field for a user.

If the user does not have a value for the specified signup field, a new value will be created.
If a value already exists, it will be updated with the provided value.

Create or update multiple signup field values for a user.

Allows setting or updating values for several signup fields in a single request.
Only the fields provided will be created or updated; unchanged fields are not included in the response.
On success, returns a 201 Created status and a Location header pointing to the list
  of all signup field values for the user.

get.description:
Retrieve the value of a specific signup field for a user.

Returns the value assigned to the specified signup field for the user.

post.description: Create multiple users in a domain with a single API call. All users are validated before processing. If any user fails validation, the entire request is rejected. The operation is queued for async processing and returns 202 Accepted immediately.

get.description:
List all group categories.

Returns a paginated list of group categories that can be used to organize student groups.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

get.description:
Retrieve a specific group category by its ID.

Returns details for a single group category.

List all groups in your organization.

Returns a paginated list of groups. You can filter by exact group name using the `name` query parameter.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter groups by exact match on group name

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

post.description:
Create a new group.

Groups can be configured with optional sign-up field rules to automate user assignment.

get.description:
Retrieve details for a specific group.

Returns group information including name, discount, user count, and sign-up field rules.

put.description:
Update group details.

Both PUT and PATCH requests perform partial updates. You can modify group name, discount,
  or sign-up field rules.

patch.description:
Update group details.

Both PUT and PATCH requests perform partial updates. You can modify group name, discount,
  or sign-up field rules.

delete.description:
Delete a group.

Permanently removes the group and all group-user associations. Users and domain users are not deleted.
This operation cannot be undone.

get.description:
List all access code pools assigned to a group.

Returns a paginated list of access code pools associated with the specified group.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

post.description:
Assign an access code pool to a group.

Specify the access code pool to add by its ID.

get.description:
Retrieve details about an access code pool assigned to a group.

Returns information about the access code pool and its association with the group.

delete.description:
Remove an access code pool from a group.

This action only removes the association; the access code pool itself is not deleted.

get.description:
List all users in a group.

Returns a paginated list of users who are members of the specified group.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

post.description:
Create a new WebPackage by providing a URL to a remotely hosted zip file.

The file will be downloaded and re-hosted in Skilljar.
The `web_package` object in the request allows you to specify properties such as title, redirect behavior,
and completion synchronization.

- `title` (required): The title for the web package. This may be overridden after processing.
- `redirect_on_completion` (optional, default: `true`)
- `sync_on_completion` (optional, default: `false`)

The response will indicate the initial state of the package.
Some fields, such as `type` and `download_url`, are not available until processing completes.

Copy and paste the URL into a new browser window if needed.

Deletion is only permitted if the WebPackage is not associated with any lessons.
If associations exist, a 400 Bad Request is returned.

post.description:
Create a new webhook for your organization.

Register a webhook to receive HTTP POST notifications when specified events occur in your Skilljar organization.
You may specify a particular event type or leave it blank to receive all event types.

- If "event_type" is null, all event types will be sent to the URL.
- If "event_type" is set, only events matching that type will be sent.
- If multiple webhooks are created with the same URL and qualify for an event, the event will be sent to the
URL only once.

delete.description:

Permanently removes the webhook and stops sending event notifications to its target URL.

post.description:
Send a test HTTP POST event of type PING to a webhook.

A failed ping will not deactivate the webhook and no retries are attempted.

seats_total.description: The maximum number of event registrants, empty if disabled. This cannot be set for sessions belonging to a multi-session event.

location.description: The location where the event will be held. This cannot be set for non-calendar sessions.

post_registration_instructions.description: HTML shown to the user after registering

event_link.description: URL allowing users to join the event. This cannot be set for non-calendar sessions.

tags.description: Comma-separated list of tags. Cannot be set on sessions attached to a multi-session event.

IltSessionRequest.description:
This serializer is a combination of ViltSession & ViltSessionEvent fields

Create/Update will write to fields for each model respectively from a flat JSON payload

instructor_email.description: Instructor email, can be provided either as instructor@example.com or Instructor Name <instructor@example.com> for events without a provider.

provider.description: The session provider. Can be one of calendar, goto.meeting, goto.training, goto.webinar, goto.webcast, webex.meeting, webex.webinar, zoom.meeting, zoom.webinar. Defaults to calendar. * `calendar` - calendar * `goto.meeting` - goto.meeting * `goto.training` - goto.training * `goto.webinar` - goto.webinar * `goto.webcast` - goto.webcast * `webex.meeting` - webex.meeting * `webex.webinar` - webex.webinar * `zoom.meeting` - zoom.meeting * `zoom.webinar` - zoom.webinar

lesson.description: The Lesson that this ViltSessionEvent belongs to. It is required to set either this or multi_session_event.

multi_session_event.description: The MultiSessionEvent that this ViltSessionEvent belongs to. It is required to set either this or lesson.

starts_at.description: ISO 8601 formatted datetime string indicating when the start of the event is scheduled

ends_at.description: ISO 8601 formatted datetime string indicating when the end of the event is scheduled

show_results_on_failure.description: If set, students who fail the quiz will be able to see the answers they submitted as well as the correct / incorrect status and feedback for each question. Correct answers will not be displayed.  (Students who pass the quiz are shown correct / incorrect status, feedback, and correct answers by default.)

max_attempts.description: By default (0), there is no limit to how many times a student may take the quiz.

require_correct_response.description: If set, the student must answer each question correctly in order to proceed to the next question. When set, there is no limit to the number of attempts to answer the question correctly.

randomize_questions.description: If set, the student will receive questions in a random order.

limit_question_count.description: By default (0), the student will have to answer every question below. Otherwise, they will receive this many questions selected at random.

randomize_answers.description: If set, the student will receive question answers in a random order (this applies to every question on the quiz).

time_limit_seconds.description: Set a time limit on the quiz. If left blank, students will have unlimited time to complete the quiz.

show_question_feedback.description: If set, students will be able to receive feedback for correct / incorrect answers on their quiz for automatically graded questions (multiple choice, multiple answer, and fill in the blank questions.) If unchecked, all feedback entered previously will be deleted.

* `DASHBOARD_TASK_CREATED` - Dashboard Task Created
